LOCAL GOVERNMENT ROUNDUP

Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee advanced a nuisance ordinance update and flagged a busy agenda for the Village Board, while the Wausau School Board met Monday to take up academic standards and facility repairs.

  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee voted unanimously to forward revised Chapter 382 Nuisances ordinance language to the Village Board for final approval.
  • The committee also reached consensus to ask Marathon County to conduct a traffic study at County Road XX and Terabonding Drive, with the existing four-way stop remaining in place.
  • Kronenwetter staff were directed to research costs and grant options for a bike and pedestrian path along Highway X, with a report due back to CLIPP in August.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board is set to consider ballot drop box installation, revisions to Chapter 41 Elections, and new oak wilt regulations at its Monday meeting.
  • The Wausau School Board is set to act on Wisconsin Academic Standards adoption, WIAA membership renewals for Wausau East, Wausau West, and WAVE, and an EFIS wall repair at South Mountain Elementary.

Coming up: Tuesday brings a full slate for Wausau, including Board of Public Works at 10:30 a.m., Finance Committee at 5:15 p.m., and Common Council at 6:30 p.m., alongside a Marathon County Public Safety Committee meeting at 5:00 p.m. Monday also features Weston's Plan Commission and Public Works Committee, both at 6:00 p.m., and the City of Wausau's Committee of the Whole at 6:30 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
